follow_up_questions: Is the AC turning on at all?
Is the fan running but no cold air coming out?
Have you checked if the temperature is set correctly?
When did you last clean or replace the air filter?

causes.fix: Clean or replace the air filter. A clogged filter blocks airflow and reduces cooling efficiency significantly.
Low refrigerant usually means a leak. Contact a certified technician to inspect and recharge refrigerant.
Clean the outdoor condenser unit. Remove debris, leaves, and dust blocking the coils.
Ensure thermostat is set to COOL mode and temperature is below current room temperature.

follow_up_questions: Is the power LED on the unit lit?
Have you checked the circuit breaker?
Is the remote control working (try replacing batteries)?
Did the unit turn off suddenly or never turned on?

causes.fix: Check your home electrical panel. Reset the breaker for the AC circuit if it has tripped.
Replace the remote control batteries with fresh AA batteries and try again.

causes.cause: Faulty power cable or loose connection
causes.fix: Check that the power cable is firmly plugged in. Inspect for any visible damage to the cord.
Unit may have shut down due to overheating or voltage fluctuation. Turn off power for 30 minutes and restart.

follow_up_questions: Is the noise coming from the indoor or outdoor unit?
Does the noise sound like rattling, buzzing, or squealing?
Did the noise start suddenly or gradually?
Is the noise constant or only when compressor kicks in?

causes.fix: Turn off the unit immediately. A loose fan blade can cause rattling. Contact a technician to inspect the fan assembly.
Turn off the unit and carefully remove any leaves, twigs, or debris from inside the outdoor condenser unit.
Inspect and tighten all accessible screws on the indoor and outdoor unit panels.

follow_up_questions: Is water dripping from the indoor unit or outdoor unit?
Is the water leak constant or only when AC is running?
Have you cleaned the drain pipe recently?
Is there ice forming on the unit?

causes.fix: Clean the condensate drain pipe. Use a wet-dry vacuum or a thin brush to remove blockage from the drain outlet.
Turn off cooling mode and run fan only for 2 hours to thaw the coil. Check filter and airflow after thawing.
The indoor unit should tilt slightly backward for proper drainage. Contact installer to re-check mounting angle.

follow_up_questions: Is the power light on?
Is the door or lid properly closed?
Have you selected a wash cycle?
Is the water supply turned on?

causes.cause: Door or lid not properly closed
causes.fix: Ensure the door is firmly shut until you hear a click. The machine will not start if the door latch is not engaged.
Check that the water inlet valve at the back of the machine is fully open. Also check the household water supply.
Check your electrical panel and reset the breaker for the washing machine circuit.
Unplug the machine for 5 minutes and plug back in to reset the control board. If issue persists contact service.

follow_up_questions: Is water still sitting in the drum after the cycle?
Can you hear the drain pump running?
Have you checked the drain hose for kinks?
When did you last clean the pump filter?

causes.fix: Locate the pump filter at the front bottom panel. Place a towel down, unscrew and clean the filter of lint and debris.
Check the drain hose at the back for kinks or bends. Ensure the hose end is not pushed too far into the standpipe.
If the pump is silent during drain cycle it may be faulty. Contact a technician to test and replace the drain pump.

follow_up_questions: Is the machine on a level surface?
Were the transit bolts removed during installation?
Is the load balanced inside the drum?
Does vibration happen throughout the cycle or only during spin?

causes.fix: Transit bolts must be removed before first use. Check the back panel for 3-4 bolts and remove them using a spanner.
Adjust the levelling feet at the bottom of the machine until it sits firmly without rocking. Use a spirit level to verify.
Open the door and redistribute clothes evenly around the drum. Avoid washing single heavy items alone.

follow_up_questions: What is the exact error code shown on the display?
Did the error appear at the start, mid-cycle, or end of cycle?
Has this error appeared before?
Did you recently change detergent or load size?

causes.fix: Check water supply valve is open. Clean the inlet filter mesh at the back of the machine where the hose connects.
Ensure door is fully closed. If error persists after closing the door latch mechanism may need replacement.
Clean the pump filter and check drain hose. Run a drain-only cycle to clear blockage.
